questions.text: Where did you grow up — what kind of place was it (city, small town, village, farm, suburb, coastal area…)?
What did the street, road or path right outside your childhood home look like?
What was your bedroom like as a child — colours on the walls, posters, bed, toys, smell?
What single toy, game, doll, bike, puzzle or object did you play with the most?
What was your favourite snack, sweet or treat you ate as a child?
What did a normal afternoon or Saturday look like when you were around 7–10 years old?
Who was your closest friend or main playmate in early childhood?
What was the funniest, silliest or most adventurous thing you and your friends ever did?
Was there a favourite outdoor spot (tree, park, creek, field, wall, empty lot…) that felt like “your place”?
Did you have any pets growing up? What were their names and what were they like?
What was one moment in childhood when you felt really proud of something you did?
Which grown-up (parent, grandparent, teacher, neighbour, aunt/uncle…) made you feel truly safe or understood?
Was there ever a time as a child when you felt scared, very sad, lonely or left out — what was happening?
What was the most magical, exciting or joyful day you remember from being under 12?
Was there a rule at home or school that felt especially unfair or hard to follow?
If you could sit down with your 8–10-year-old self for five minutes, what would you say to comfort or guide them?
What part of your childhood do you now realise had the biggest influence on who you became?
Was there a childhood friend, cousin or family member you were very close to but later lost touch with — what happened?
What childhood experience or belief do you now see very differently as an adult?
Is there one specific childhood memory that still brings up strong feelings (happy, bittersweet, painful) when you think about it today?
Looking back, what one thing from your childhood would you most want your own children or grandchildren to experience?
Which childhood moment taught you the biggest early lesson about yourself or about other people?
What is one childhood experience you now regret — and what would you do differently if you could?
Looking back, what one person from your childhood do you wish you had thanked, forgiven or said something important to?
What do you hope people who knew you as a child would remember about the person you were?
